entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The general crop species being studied.

entities.name: ORYZA SATIVA
entities.description: The recurrent parent species used in the interspecific cross.

entities.name: ORYZA GLABERRIMA
entities.description: The donor parent species used in the interspecific cross.

entities.name: WAB 56-104
entities.description: A tropical japonica variety of Oryza sativa used as the recurrent parent.

entities.name: CG 14
entities.description: A variety of Oryza glaberrima used as the donor parent.

entities.name: NERICA
entities.description: Interspecific inbred progeny derived from crosses between Oryza sativa and Oryza glaberrima.

entities.name: ANTHER CULTURE
entities.description: A technique used to derive doubled haploids from BC2 lines.

entities.name: DOUBLED HAPLOIDS
entities.description: Lines produced via anther culture to achieve homozygosity.

entities.name: INBREEDING
entities.description: The process of mating closely related individuals for eight generations to fix lines.

entities.name: MICROSATELLITE MARKERS
entities.description: Genetic markers used to examine the relative contribution of parents and genetic differences.

entities.name: CLUSTER ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to group the interspecific lines.

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COMPONENT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to detect major groups in the materials.

entities.name: GENETIC DIFFERENCES
entities.description: The extent of variation in genomic composition among the sister lines.

entities.name: NON-PARENTAL ALLELES
entities.description: Alleles present in the progeny that were not found in either parent.
